EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A slow moving Pacific trough produced 10 to 20 inches of snow.  The exception was 23 inches at Geyser Pass in the La Sal Mountains.

A cold and moist upper low moved slowly eastward across the southern Rockies and produced 12 to 16 inches of snow in the mountains of extreme eastern Utah.  A heavier reported amount was 21 inches near Trout Creek in the eastern Uinta Mountains.  Winds of 40 to 50 MPH in exposed areas produced blowing and drifting snow.

An approaching cold front induced strong winds across eastern Utah, with gusts of 50 to 60 MPH common.  Some peak measured winds include 69 MPH at Bryson Canyon on the Tavaputs Plateau, 61 MPH at Canyonlands Airport north of Moab, 61 MPH at the 7000 foot level in the La Sal Mountains, 52 MPH at Bluff, and 52 MPH in the eastern Uinta Mountains.The strong winds downed some trees and disrupted power at some locations.

This tornado was actually a gustnado.  The gustnado travelled across a drill rig site which was near the Slick Rock Campground.  Fifteen people were at the drill rig site and took cover when they saw the gustnado approaching.  The gustnado picked up rocks and other loose items, breaking out windows on a drill rig enclosure and a nearby truck.  The gustnado continued on down the hill and uprooted several trees before dissipating.

A series of fast moving weather disturbances brought a period of prolonged snowfall to much of eastern Utah.  Heavy snow with winds gusting to over 50 mph occurred with this storm.  Most mountain locations received 6 to 10 inches of snow, although 11 inches fell at Cedar Point.  Some lower elevations over southeast Utah also reported heavy snowfall amounts, including 8 inches at Natural Bridges National Monument.

A cold upper low from the north produced the first significant snowfall of the season in extreme northeast Utah.  6 to 10 inches of snow fell on the mountains above the 8000 foot level.
Lightning sparked a wildfire which consumed 10 acres.

A slow moving thunderstorm dropped heavy rain over Moab, UT. resulting in minor flooding of streets and creeks in and around Moab.  Portions of highway 191 near 400 East Street were covered in red sand and mud.  The Utah Highway Department of Transportation cleared the road of the debris after the storm had moved through the area.
Lightning blew out a transmitter at a radio station.

Lightning struck the northeast corner of a roof of an apartment complex near an elevator shaft.  The lightning resulted in a fire which caused significant damage to the building's elevator, electrical, phone, and alarm systems.  Nineteen residents were forced to evacuate the building as a result of the fire.

Lightning struck the roof of an apartment complex near the flashing on a parapet wall.  The lightning traveled along the flashing and down a metal vent pipe into the inner portion of the parapet wall igniting a fire between the ceiling and the roof.

CZ_NAME: P NC WEBER/P C MORGAN/P W C SUMMIT/P SC WASATCH
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The new Millennium arrived with a snowy bang, as a series of winter storms pounded much of the state in a three day period. This was on the heels of a very dry start to winter and the ski season, so it was much appreciated. The brunt of the snow fell in the Northern mountains and along the Wasatch Front. Over 2 feet of fresh powder was common at the ski resorts, with 8-12 inches along the benches of Northern Utah and 4-8 the norm in most valleys. Some higher amounts included Alta with 33 inches, Snowbird with 30 inches, and Spruces with 22 inches. East Layton picked up 11 inches of snow, with Upper Millcreek checking in with 10 inches. The icy roads caused a few small accidents, with only minor injuries.

CZ_NAME: C W BOX ELDER/C W XE TOOLE/X W SALT LAKE/X NW UTAH
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another round of snow and cold moved into Northern Utah, with 4-10 inches in most valleys. The mountains picked up 1 to 1 1/2 feet of fresh powder. The snow made for hazardous driving conditions, causing a tractor-trailer to jackknife and lose control on I-15 in Riverdale. This caused a chain reaction, leading to over 50 other cars running into one another. Six people were hurt and taken to local hospitals. All of the injuries turned out to be minor.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced a wind gust of 66mph (57kts) at the St. George Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow fell in portions of the Wasatch Mountains, with Alta picking up 11 inches of snow, and Brighton and Solitude 10 inches. Down south, Brian Head reported 9 inches of new snow.

CZ_NAME: W SUMMIT T NW/SE SALT LAKE/WASATCH X SC/E UTAH/P X E JUAB/X NW SAN PETE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A wintery blast dropped heavy snow and produced blizzard-like conditions in the mountains. The storm also brought thunder and lightning with the cold front, with heavy snow making driving hazardous. On Highway 89 in South Willard, the snowy roads caused a head-on collision that killed a 31-year-old man. The driver of the other car was seriously injured. Snowfall in the Northern Mountains ranged from 12-18 inches, with 4-10 inches in some of the higher mountain valleys. Very strong winds accompanied the snow, with Mt. Baldy at Alta recording a gust to 114 mph (99 kts). Other gusts included 84 mph (73 kts) at Hidden Peak at Snowbird, 71 mph (62 kts) at Deer Creek Dam, and 61 mph (53 kts) at the Provo Airport.

Another decent storm moved through Northern Utah, producing 10-20 inches in the mountains with 3-8 inches elsewhere. Five people were hurt in an eight-car pileup on I-15. Alta picked up 21 inches of fresh powder, with 17 inches at Snowbird.
Heavy snow fell in portions of the Wasatch Mountains, with Alta picking up 11 inches of snow, and Brighton and Solitude 10 inches. Down south, Brian Head reported 9 inches of new snow.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Localized heavy rains fell in the Zion National Park area, producing some flash floods on Pine Creek and in portions of The Narrows. Two parties of 4 hikers were trapped near The Narrows, and were forced to stay overnight as the waters receded. They hiked out the following afternoon and were rescued.
A funnel cloud was spotted in Bountiful near the temple. It retreated back into the cloud after a few minutes.
A severe thunderstorm dumped 3/4 inch hail on the town of Orderville.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A bewitching storm brought scary amounts of snow to many of the ski resorts and portions of central Utah. The storm started early on the 30th and continued into Halloween. Snow totals from this event included 35 inches at Alta, 27 inches and Brian Head, 18 inches in Scofield, 14 inches in Kanosh, 13 inches at Fillmore and 12 inches in Delta.

Quarter-size hail and winds estimated at 40 to 50 MPH were reported by an amateur radio operator traveling on eastbound Interstate 70.  At a minimum, the hail dented the RV being driven by the amateur radio operator.
A weak tornado was observed by a radio station employee and her husband as they traveled north on US Highway 191.  The thunderstorm which produced this tornado had a dark green appearance.

CZ_NAME: C W BOX ELDER/C W XE TOOLE/X W SALT LAKE/X NW UTAH
EVENT_NARRATIVE: South winds blew ahead of a cold front over much of Western Utah. Gusts from 60-80mph (52-70kts) were clocked at several locations in Tooele County. Several tree limbs and some roof shingles were damaged in the city of Tooele.
A lightning strike near Myton damaged a treatment unit and destroyed two 400-gallon oil tanks.
Microburst winds blew through portions of Beaver County, with Brimstone Reservoir reporting a gust to 64mph (56kts).
Over 2 and a half inches of rain in 2 hours caused flash flooding in New Harmony. All washes in the area were running fast and full. Pea to marble-sized hail also was reported with this storm.

Slow moving thunderstorms formed over Cedar City, causing massive flash flooding. The west side of town received the worst of the flooding. Drainage systems in town were overwhelmed by the storm, as flood waters up to 4 feet deep moved through the streets. One subdivision was evacuated as a wall of water broke through windows and flooded basements. Mud and debris covered Highway 56 west of town, and some intersections were washed out. Several vehicles were also swept down city streets. Two traffic accidents were also caused by the heavy rain and flooding. Rain totals were impressive with one spotter on the west side of town reporting 2.36 inches during the storm. Miraculously, nobody was injured.
Lightning struck a home in Layton, starting a fire that gutted out the attic. The fire caused $100,000 worth of damage.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains around fell over a burn area on the east slopes of the Oquirrh Mountains, resulting in a flash flood of water, mud, debris and ash. The flood covered a few yards in the Lakepoint area. Six baby rabbits were lost to the water.

CZ_NAME: W UTAH T X NW/P X E JUAB
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong, gusty winds blew near the mouth of Hobble Creek Canyon in the east portions of Springville. the winds damaged some trees, blew shingles of of some roofs, and damaged a carport.
A thunderstorm produced a dry Microburst wind gust of 59 mph (51 kts) at the Wendover Airport.

The icy roads caused a few small accidents, with only minor injuries.

CZ_NAME: E WASATCH T X NE/S WC DUCHESNE/X NE SAN PETE/CARBON T SC/P X NE X NW EMERY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The new Millennium arrived with a snowy bang, as a series of winter storms pounded much of the state in a three day period. This was on the heels of a very dry start to winter and the ski season, so it was much appreciated. The brunt of the snow fell in the Northern mountains and along the Wasatch Front. Over 2 feet of fresh powder was common at the ski resorts, with 8-12 inches along the benches of Northern Utah and 4-8 the norm in most valleys. Some higher amounts included Alta with 33 inches, Snowbird with 30 inches, and Spruces with 22 inches. East Layton picked up 11 inches of snow, with Upper Millcreek checking in with 10 inches. The icy roads caused a few small accidents, with only minor injuries.

CZ_NAME: W SUMMIT T NW/SE SALT LAKE/WASATCH X SC/E UTAH/P X E JUAB/X NW SAN PETE
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two Salt Lake County teenagers were buried in an avalanche north of Alta in Flagstaff Gully. One of the teens was riding a sled while the other waited below taking photographs. The sledder came off a cornice ridge, triggering the avalanche. The sledder was partially buried to his shoulders but managed to dig himself out. The other teen below was buried completely fro about 15 minutes. When the teen and other skiers who had gathered found the buried boy he was unconscious and not breathing. The teen was revived and airlifted to University Hospital, where he was treated for neck injuries and hypothermia.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong downslope winds developed overnight across Weber County. Wind gusts estimated at 115mph (100kts) tore the roofs off of 3 condominiums in South Ogden. Major damage was also caused to the interiors of these condos, along with some toppled garage doors, a toppled brick wall, and a damaged fence nearby. Miraculously, nobody was seriously injured. Damage estimates to the area was just over 1 million dollars. These same downslope winds also caused an estimated 70 thousand dollars damage to the roof of Wasatch Hall on the campus of Weber State University, and several thousand dollars in damage due to scattered losses of fences, shingles and trees in the Ogden area. These same downslope winds in Centerville were estimated at 100mph (87kts). The wind derailed 19 cars of a 48-car train, with 13 cars being toppled over. Over 400 feet of track had to be replaced. Again, nobody was hurt. Two semis were also blown over in Centerville, where actual winds were clocked at 86mph (75kts). In the Kaysville-Layton area, the winds blew swamp coolers off of roofs, flattened trees, and damaged a garage door. In Salt Lake County, the winds uprooted and damaged several trees in Sandy and Holladay. Still, no serious injuries were reported. In East Carbon County, a 70 foot single-wide trailer was picked up and thrown onto another building along Highway 123, by winds estimated at 75mph (65kts). Several power poles were also blown down. In Sunnyside, an 86-year-old woman was injured when she opened a door, which was caught by the wind. She was thrown against a wall, suffering a cut finger and bruised hips. The wind also damaged a roof and porch of an abandoned boarding house in Columbia. In Santa Clara strong winds estimated at around 80mph (70 kts) blew over several trees, a few road signs, along with some fences and sheds.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two separate thunderstorms dumped 3/4 inch and 1 inch diameter hail in Henrieville.

A bewitching storm brought scary amounts of snow to many of the ski resorts and portions of central Utah. The storm started early on the 30th and continued into Halloween. Snow totals from this event included 35 inches at Alta, 27 inches and Brian Head, 18 inches in Scofield, 14 inches in Kanosh, 13 inches at Fillmore and 12 inches in Delta.

CZ_NAME: P NC WEBER/P C MORGAN/P W C SUMMIT/P SC WASATCH
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast moving cold front dropped some heavy snow in the Northern Utah Mountains. 1-2 feet was quite common. Some of the heaviest amounts were 24 inches at Alta, 20 inches at Snowbird and 13 inches at Solitude.

CZ_NAME: NE BOX ELDER T X NE/W WEBER/DAVIS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A wintery blast dropped heavy snow and produced blizzard-like conditions in the mountains. The storm also brought thunder and lightning with the cold front, with heavy snow making driving hazardous. On Highway 89 in South Willard, the snowy roads caused a head-on collision that killed a 31-year-old man. The driver of the other car was seriously injured. Snowfall in the Northern Mountains ranged from 12-18 inches, with 4-10 inches in some of the higher mountain valleys. Very strong winds accompanied the snow, with Mt. Baldy at Alta recording a gust to 114 mph (99 kts). Other gusts included 84 mph (73 kts) at Hidden Peak at Snowbird, 71 mph (62 kts) at Deer Creek Dam, and 61 mph (53 kts) at the Provo Airport.

CZ_NAME: P NC WEBER/P C MORGAN/P W C SUMMIT/P SC WASATCH
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another decent storm moved through Northern Utah, producing 10-20 inches in the mountains with 3-8 inches elsewhere. Five people were hurt in an eight-car pileup on I-15. Alta picked up 21 inches of fresh powder, with 17 inches at Snowbird.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong south winds in advance of a cold front blew across much of Utah, with a gust to 90mph (78kts) recorded at Historic farm in the Cache Valley. The wind broke some tree branches, knocked over a few fences, broke several windows, and toppled a grain shed. No injuries were reported.

CZ_NAME: RICH/CACHE T NW/P E C WEBER/MOST OF MORGAN/NW SUMMIT/NE SALT LAKE/X NE BOX ELDER

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A hefty little storm moved through Southern Utah, dropping 1-2 feet of fresh powder over the high country. Brian Head received 26 inches of snow, with 18 inches at Midway and 14 inches at Bryce Canyon National Park.

The slide from Square Top Peak was estimated to be near 500 feet wide and 5 feet deep, with a vertical drop of 1000 feet. The couple, a man and a woman, were discovered the next day by a search team.

CZ_NAME: RICH/CACHE T NW/P E C WEBER/MOST OF MORGAN/NW SUMMIT/NE SALT LAKE/X NE BOX ELDER

Other big numbers included Solitude with 32 inches, Snowbird with 30 inches, Brighton with 28 inches, and Sundance with 24 inches.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A shift to a more westerly flow brought the lake effect snows northward into Northern Davis and Southern Weber Counties. Hill Air Force Base reported 10 inches of snow, with other areas from Layton to Ogden receiving 6-9 inches.

A sudden downburst from a thunderstorm produced flash flooding down the gorges of the hills on the west side of Aurora. The water soon filled the canals that go through town. The canal bank broke in 3 places, causing the State road department to be called out to repair the damage. The Denmark Wash overflowed a road leading to some homes, and flooded some farmyards. No injuries or damage was reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low pressure system dropped slowly south over eastern Utah.  This resulted in heavy snow over the eastern Uinta Mountains where 15 to 19 inches of new snow was reported. Significant snowfall also developed over the lower elevations of east-central Utah where 2 to 5 inches of snow was common.

Some of the higher snowfall totals included 20 inches at Alta, 14 inches in Silver Fork, 12 inches at Chalk Creek, Monte Cristo and Snowbird, and 11 inches in Woodland.

CZ_NAME: RICH/CACHE T NW/P E C WEBER/MOST OF MORGAN/NW SUMMIT/NE SALT LAKE/X NE BOX ELDER
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful cold front brought strong winds to Northern Utah. A 38-year-old woman was killed near her home in Brigham City. As she got out of her car and walked to her house, an 80-year-old tree was blown over, crushing her car, then landing on and killing the woman near her front porch.  Some of the stronger gusts were 64mph (56kts) in Wendover, 63mph (55kts) in Logan and Sandy, and 61 mph (53kts) in Vernon.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm downburst produced a 69mph (60kts) wind gust at the St. George Airport.

CZ_NAME: RICH/CACHE T NW/P E C WEBER/MOST OF MORGAN/NW SUMMIT/NE SALT LAKE/X NE BOX ELDER
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A white Christmas was assured over much of Northern Utah, as Mother Nature brought a nice dose of snow on Christmas Eve. Most of the snow was over early Christmas Morning. Some of the higher amounts included 11 inches at Snowbird, 10 inches at Alta, 8 inches at Solitude and in North Salt Lake, 6 inches in Centerville and 4 inches in East Layton.

CZ_NAME: W SUMMIT T NW/SE SALT LAKE/WASATCH X SC/E UTAH/P X E JUAB/X NW SAN PETE

CZ_NAME: RICH/CACHE T NW/P E C WEBER/MOST OF MORGAN/NW SUMMIT/NE SALT LAKE/X NE BOX ELDER
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow pounded much of Northern Utah as a cold trough settled over the Great Basin. The Northern Utah Valleys along the Wasatch Front picked up 3-8 inches of snow, while the mountains received anywhere from 8-13 inches of powder.
Heavy rain from slow moving thunderstorms resulted in minor street flooding and triggered a few small rock slides.

CZ_NAME: RICH/CACHE T NW/P E C WEBER/MOST OF MORGAN/NW SUMMIT/NE SALT LAKE/X NE BOX ELDER
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An avalanche occurred at the Snowbasin Resort in the Strawberry Bowl area around noon. It was about 100 feet wide and 150 feet long with a vertical drop of 400 feet. Five skiers were caught in the slide, with 2 being buried and the others partially buried. The two buried were quickly dug out, suffering only minor injuries. The others escaped without injury.

CZ_NAME: RICH/CACHE T NW/P E C WEBER/MOST OF MORGAN/NW SUMMIT/NE SALT LAKE/X NE BOX ELDER
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A ski patroller from Snowbasin was seriously injured in an avalanche in a closed area of Snowbasin. The man was doing routine checks of the Mt. Ogden flank in-bounds when a slab of wind-blown snow 200 feet wide slid 300 feet down the mountain. He was able to ride out the slide, but was slammed into a tree, breaking some ribs and his jaw, and causing other internal injuries.
Severe thunderstorms brought 3/4-1 inch hail to the Centerville area, damaging tree limbs along with a few gardens.

A 26-year-old man and a 27-year-old man were struck and killed by lightning while fighting a fire on the north end of the Stansbury Mountains. The lightning strike also injured 4 other members of the Flame-N-Go's firefighting unit from the Utah State Prison's Lone Peak facility. The leader of the crew was not struck, but suffered from extreme shock.
A thunderstorm spawned a funnel cloud that was witnessed by several people near the Overlake Subdivision on the north end of Tooele.

Heavy rains on the north end of the Oquirrh Mountains brought a river of mud, ash, and water into some yards in Lake Point. The rains fell over an area which had lost the erosion-controlling vegetation from a fire back in July. Up to six inches of mud and ash covered the yards.
Heavy rain brought flooding in the Saratoga Springs area, with 2 homes having their basements flooded. Some road damage was also reported with the flooding.
Severe thunderstorms produced heavy rain and 3/4 inch hail in Lindon.
Strong thunderstorm winds in Sandy sent a large tree onto several cars, and knocked other trees onto a house and several power lines.
A severe thunderstorm deposited 1 1/2 inch hail on the town of Herriman.
A dry thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 61 mph (53 kts) at the airport in Wendover.

A 47-year-old male British tourist was struck by lightning while standing under a tree a Bryce Canyon National Park. The man suffered head injuries and superficial burns. He was flown to LDS Hospital in Salt Lake City where he was in critical condition. He later recovered from his injuries.
A severe thunderstorm dropped 3/4 inch hail on the Bountiful bench.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy monsoonal rains were widespread over Northern Utah. The heaviest rain fell in Weber, Davis and Morgan Counties, with amounts generally between 3.00 and 3.70 inches. There were numerous reports of flooding, with the hardest hit areas being Roy, Kaysville, Ogden, and North Salt Lake. A number of homes reported water in their basements, with storm sewers and streets becoming overwhelmed by the deluge. Some homes in Roy had sewage back up into their basements as well. All-in-all, it was a giant mess.

CZ_NAME: RICH/CACHE T NW/P E C WEBER/MOST OF MORGAN/NW SUMMIT/NE SALT LAKE/X NE BOX ELDER
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A nice late March snowstorm dumped up to a foot of snow along mainly the northern portions of the Wasatch Front. Some of the higher totals included 12 inches in South Weber, 9 inches at Snowbird and Alta, 8 inches in East Layton and South Ogden, and 7 inches in Clearfield.

CZ_NAME: C W BOX ELDER/C W XE TOOLE/X W SALT LAKE/X NW UTAH
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds in advance of a cold front turned over 2 semi-tractor trailers between Grantsville and Wendover on I-80. Nobody was injured.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An 11-year-old girl was killed and six other children were injured when lightning struck them as they were getting out of Midvalley Elementary School in Midvale. The children were walking across the playground, heading for their bus when the lightning struck. The victim was still alive as she was transferred to the hospital, but died later from her injuries. One other child was hospitalized, but recovered. The other five children suffered minor injuries. Shortly afterwards, also in Midvale, a 36-year-old man was injured by lightning as he ran out to his car to roll up his windows. His injuries were not life-threatening.

A severe thunderstorm spawned a small tornado as it made its way through Sanpete County. An eyewitness spotted the F0 tornado as it touched down just south of Highway 89, about 5 miles east of Gunnison. It crossed the highway, pelting the eyewitnesses car with mud and debris and causing some power lines to arc. The tornado was on the ground for only a few minutes. As the thunderstorm continued to track northward, Manti clocked 60mph (52kts) winds, with tree damage noted at the LDS Temple in town. Trees were also blown over in Spring City and Mt. Pleasant. There was also some damage to one home and a city building in Mt. Pleasant from the thunderstorm winds. Several turkey barns were also damaged in Fairview.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm dropped 3/4 inch hail in Levan and produced estimated wind gusts of 50-60mph.

A small tornado touched down in the Salt Lake City suburb of Holladay in the early evening hours. The F0 tornado ripped apart a sheet metal roof on the receiving dock of an Albertson's Supermarket, and a Goodyear Tire Store reported minor damage to the roof and a sign. Several cars at the tire store had their windows blown out as well. Luckily, there were no injuries reported.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 300 foot wide avalanche near Bridal Veil Falls briefly damned the Provo River. There were no injuries and no damage from the slide.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A nice late March snowstorm dumped up to a foot of snow along mainly the northern portions of the Wasatch Front. Some of the higher totals included 12 inches in South Weber, 9 inches at Snowbird and Alta, 8 inches in East Layton and South Ogden, and 7 inches in Clearfield.
Thunderstorms moving through Davis and Salt Lake Counties dropped pea to 3/8 inch hail at a few spots, including Kaysville, West Jordon, the Salt Lake International Airport, and Sandy.

An 11-year-old girl was killed and six other children were injured when lightning struck them as they were getting out of Midvalley Elementary School in Midvale. The children were walking across the playground, heading for their bus when the lightning struck. The victim was still alive as she was transferred to the hospital, but died later from her injuries. One other child was hospitalized, but recovered. The other five children suffered minor injuries. Shortly afterwards, also in Midvale, a 36-year-old man was injured by lightning as he ran out to his car to roll up his windows. His injuries were not life-threatening.

A severe thunderstorm spawned a small tornado as it made its way through Sanpete County. An eyewitness spotted the F0 tornado as it touched down just south of Highway 89, about 5 miles east of Gunnison. It crossed the highway, pelting the eyewitnesses car with mud and debris and causing some power lines to arc. The tornado was on the ground for only a few minutes. As the thunderstorm continued to track northward, Manti clocked 60mph (52kts) winds, with tree damage noted at the LDS Temple in town. Trees were also blown over in Spring City and Mt. Pleasant. There was also some damage to one home and a city building in Mt. Pleasant from the thunderstorm winds. Several turkey barns were also damaged in Fairview.

CZ_NAME: C E SUMMIT/X NE WASATCH/N DUCHESNE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter-like storm brought an early taste of things to come, producing widespread snow in the Northern Mountains, with brisk east winds along west slopes of the Wasatch Front. Over a foot of snow fell in many mountain locations, with smaller amounts in the mountain valleys. Some of the higher totals included 14 inches at Henefer, Snowbird and Woodruff, 12 inches at Park City and Randolph, 10 inches at Coalville and The Canyons,  8 inches in Peoa, 6 inches in Enterprise, and 5 inches in Morgan and on the Tooele Bench.

CZ_NAME: NE BOX ELDER T X NE/W WEBER/DAVIS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful cold front brought strong winds to Northern Utah. A 38-year-old woman was killed near her home in Brigham City. As she got out of her car and walked to her house, an 80-year-old tree was blown over, crushing her car, then landing on and killing the woman near her front porch.  Some of the stronger gusts were 64mph (56kts) in Wendover, 63mph (55kts) in Logan and Sandy, and 61 mph (53kts) in Vernon.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm dumped 3/4 inch hail on the town of Circleville.
A tornado was spotted near Panguitch on Highway 20 which goes from US-89 to I-15. The tornado stayed over open country for about 5 minutes before lifting. No damage was reported.
A thunderstorm dropped 3/4 inch hail in the towns of Fillmore and Meadow.

CZ_NAME: W SUMMIT T NW/SE SALT LAKE/WASATCH X SC/E UTAH/P X E JUAB/X NW SAN PETE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter-like storm brought an early taste of things to come, producing widespread snow in the Northern Mountains, with brisk east winds along west slopes of the Wasatch Front. Over a foot of snow fell in many mountain locations, with smaller amounts in the mountain valleys. Some of the higher totals included 14 inches at Henefer, Snowbird and Woodruff, 12 inches at Park City and Randolph, 10 inches at Coalville and The Canyons,  8 inches in Peoa, 6 inches in Enterprise, and 5 inches in Morgan and on the Tooele Bench.

More heavy snow for the Wasatch Mountains, with light to moderate amounts in the lower elevations over the 3 day period. Heavier storm totals included 44 inches at Snowbird, 38 inches at Alta, 36 inches at Brian Head, 33 inches at Solitude, and 39 inches at Brighton. The Wasatch Front valleys and benches picked up 4-10 inches of snow for the most part.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Monsoon rains brought widespread flooding to nearly all of Washington County. At least one home was flooded in Dammeron Valley when a drainage ditch spilled over its banks. The Veyo pool sustained mud and silt damage when flood waters swept through the facility. I Brookside, a UP&L canal started leaking and washed out a road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy monsoonal rains were widespread over Northern Utah. The heaviest rain fell in Weber, Davis and Morgan Counties, with amounts generally between 3.00 and 3.70 inches. There were numerous reports of flooding, with the hardest hit areas being Roy, Kaysville, Ogden, and North Salt Lake. A number of homes reported water in their basements, with storm sewers and streets becoming overwhelmed by the deluge. Some homes in Roy had sewage back up into their basements as well. All-in-all, it was a giant mess.

CZ_NAME: NE BOX ELDER T X NE/W WEBER/DAVIS
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong Easterly canyon winds developed early in the morning along mainly the Northern Wasatch Front. Some higher gusts were 66mph (57kts) on the Ogden bench, 63mph (55kts) in Brigham City, and 61mph (52kts) at Hill AFB.

CZ_NAME: RICH/CACHE T NW/P E C WEBER/MOST OF MORGAN/NW SUMMIT/NE SALT LAKE/X NE BOX ELDER
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Another shot of snow for the northern ski areas, with Solitude getting 15 inches, Brighton, Snowbird and Sundance with 14 inches and Alta getting 13 inches.

CZ_NAME: RICH/CACHE T NW/P E C WEBER/MOST OF MORGAN/NW SUMMIT/NE SALT LAKE/X NE BOX ELDER
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A major winter storm blasted Northern Utah with heavy, wet mountain snow and valley rain and snow. Snowfall of 2-3 feet was common in the mountains, dramatically increasing the avalanche danger. Snow reports included 34-36 inches at Alta, 31 inches at Solitude, 29 inches at Snowbird, 28 inches at Powder Mountain, 27 inches at Brighton, Snowbasin and Sundance,  and 19 inches at Deer Valley.
